name: DateRangePicker

unions.name: CalendarType
unions.comment: The type which specifies what size calendar you want to display

unions.name: ClockStyle
unions.comment: Represents if times should be displayed in 24 hours or 12 hour format

unions.name: DatePicker
unions.comment: A record which represents the main datepicker model

unions.name: DatePickerVisibility
unions.comment: The type of that represents the current visibility of the date picker

unions.name: DateSelectionType
unions.comment: Represents if a single date or a range of dates should be selected.

unions.name: Format
unions.comment: The type which specifies if a user had specified a time in the input box as well as the selected date.

unions.name: Interval
unions.comment: The type of interval for a custom preset

unions.name: Msg
unions.comment: An opaque type representing messages that are passed inside the DatePicker.

unions.name: PresetType
unions.comment:
The type of that represents available presets that can be put in a preset menu.

There are a couple premade presets, but you can create a CustomPreset of your own as well.

unions.name: Selection
unions.comment:
The type which represents what the current selection is in the datepicker.

If you select a preset you can use @presetToPartsRange to get the appropriate posix range for the selection.

aliases.name: Config
aliases.comment: A record which specifies config options which can be set when initializes the datepicker
aliases.type: { allowedRange : DateRangePicker.PartsRange, presets : List.List DateRangePicker.PresetType, calendarType : DateRangePicker.CalendarType, datepickerVisibility : DateRangePicker.DatePickerVisibility, languageConfig : DateRangePicker.LanguageConfig, dateSelectionType : DateRangePicker.DateSelectionType, canChooseTime : Basics.Bool, hidePresets : Basics.Bool, defaultSelection : Maybe.Maybe DateRangePicker.Selection, displayTimezone : Time.Zone, displayDate : Maybe.Maybe Time.Posix, clockStyle : DateRangePicker.ClockStyle, yPadding : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int }

aliases.name: CustomPreset
aliases.comment:
A record that represents a user created custom preset

A custom preset represents how to select a range of posix given today's date.
aliases.type: { intervalStart : DateRangePicker.Interval, intervalStartValue : Basics.Int, intervalEnd : DateRangePicker.Interval, intervalEndValue : Basics.Int, display : String.String }

aliases.name: LanguageConfig
aliases.comment: A record that can be used if a language other than english is wanted.
aliases.type: { done : String.String, reset : String.String, datePickerInputPlaceholder : String.String, dateRangePickerInputPlaceholder : String.String, presets : String.String, today : String.String, yesterday : String.String, pastWeek : String.String, pastMonth : String.String, pastYear : String.String, includeTimeTitle : String.String, dateFormatLanguage : DateFormat.Language.Language, beforeThisDate : String.String, afterThisDate : String.String, am : String.String, pm : String.String, to : String.String, displayFormat : List.List DateFormat.Token }

aliases.name: PartsRange
aliases.comment: Holds the beginning and end of the selection

aliases.name: PosixRange
aliases.comment: The type of that represents a range of two posix times.

values.name: defaultConfig
values.comment: A default config which can be combined with @initWithOptions so that you only need to specify the fields which you want to customize

values.name: defaultOpener
values.comment:
A pre-made opener that can be used to open the datepicker instead of @open.

    It will need an id so that the datepicker can dynamically be placed.

    Html.map DatePickerMsgs <| defaultOpener model.datePicker openerId

You will need to call convert the message to the appropriate type via Html.map

values.name: defaultPresets
values.comment: The defaults for the preset buttons

values.name: displayFormat
values.comment: Gets the current selection format

values.name: displaySelection
values.comment: A helper function to display the selection in the same way that the datepicker does

values.name: displaygetSelection
values.comment: A helper function to display the selection in the same way that the datepicker does. It is in utc time.

values.name: englishLanguageConfig
values.comment: The default english language

values.name: focusInput
values.comment: focuses the inputbox

values.name: getConfig
values.comment: Gets the current languageConfig

values.name: getEndOfDayParts
values.comment: Sets all of the time parts to their logical end except for milliseconds

values.name: getSelection
values.comment: Get the current selection in local time.

values.name: getSelectionRange
values.comment: A convenience function to get the current selection as a parts range in local time.

values.name: getStartOfDayParts
values.comment: sets all of the timne parts to 0

values.name: hasRangeChanged
values.comment: Check if the selection has changed.

values.name: hasSelectionChanged
values.comment: Check if the selection has changed.

values.name: initWithOptions
values.comment: Initialize the datepicker with the default settings

values.name: isOpen
values.comment: Checks if the datepicker is open

values.name: languageConfig
values.comment: Gets the current languageConfig

values.name: open
values.comment:
A helper attribute which allows you to open the datepicker using any html element.

    The element you place this attribute on has to have an id so that the datepicker can dynamically be placed.

    Html.map DatePickerMsgs <| button [Html.Attribute.id openerId, open openerId ] [ text "Open Me!" ]

You will need to call convert the message to the appropriate type via Html.map

values.name: openMsg
values.comment: The open date picker msg so you can open the date picker with a custom event

values.name: partsRangeToPosixRange
values.comment: conversion from partsrnage to posix range

values.name: presetToDisplayString
values.comment: A helper function to get the display value for a given preset

values.name: presetToPartsRange
values.comment: Converts a preset to a selectable value

values.name: presets
values.comment: Gets the current presets

values.name: resetModel
values.comment: Change the datePicker's current config. This will reset changed state.

values.name: selectPreset
values.comment: A helper function to set a preset outside of the default preset ui

values.name: setCalendarType
values.comment: A helper function to change the calendar type on an existing model. Usually you should use @initWithOptions and configure this at initialization.

values.name: setDisplayFormat
values.comment: sets the current selection format

values.name: setOpen
values.comment: Set whether or not the datepicker is open. Usually you should use @open or @defaultOpener to manage this.

values.name: setSelection
values.comment: Sets the datepicker's selection outside of the ui. Normally the ui should have all the interactions you would want.

values.name: subscriptions
values.comment:
The subscriptions for the datepicker

    Sub.map DatePickerMsgs <|
        DateRangePicker.subscriptions model.datePicker

values.name: update
values.comment:
The update for the datepicker. You will need to integrate this into your own update.

    DatePickerMsgs msg_ ->
        let
            ( newDateRangePicker, dateRangePickerCmd ) =
                DateRangePicker.update msg_ model.datePicker
        in
        ( { model | datePicker = newDateRangePicker }, Cmd.map DatePickerMsgs dateRangePickerCmd )

values.name: updateModelWithConfig
values.comment: Change the datePicker's current config. This will reset changed state.

values.name: view
values.comment:
The view for the datepicker. You will have to pass in the current time as well as the local zone and the datepicker model.

    DateRangePicker.view datePicker
